title: MLS: multi-group support in MlsClient (HashMap of groups)
description:
What: Parameterize MlsClient to support multiple simultaneous named groups.

Algorithm:
1. Replace Option<MlsGroup> field with HashMap<Vec<u8>, MlsGroup> keyed by group_id bytes
2. Add group_id-parameterized methods:
   - create_group_with_id(group_id: &[u8]) -> Result<()>
   - add_member_to_group(group_id: &[u8], kp_bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)>
   - remove_member_from_group(group_id: &[u8], pub_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<u8>>
   - join_from_welcome_for_group(group_id: &[u8], welcome: &[u8]) -> Result<()>
   - encrypt_for_group(group_id: &[u8], plaintext: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>>
   - process_for_group(group_id: &[u8], bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>>
   - has_group_id(group_id: &[u8]) -> bool
   - group_contains_id(group_id: &[u8], pub_id: &str) -> bool
   - epoch_for_group(group_id: &[u8]) -> Option<u64>
3. Keep all existing methods (create_group, add_member, encrypt, process_incoming, etc.) as wrappers using b'nie-room' default group_id
4. Update room_hpke_keypair() to optionally accept group_id (default b'nie-room')
5. All existing tests must still pass (backward compat is mandatory)

Defensive rules:
- Return Err('no such group: ...') not panic when group_id not in HashMap
- Never log group_id bytes as if they were text
- No unwrap() on HashMap lookup in production paths

Team: standard R+I+T
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-core passes including all existing MLS tests; two-group roundtrip test: message to group A does not decrypt in group B
